summary:
Obstructive sleep apnea causes chronic hypoxia and disrupted sleep, impairing restorative recovery and reducing cardiorespiratory efficiency. These changes lead to diminished aerobic capacity and overall decline in physical endurance.  

Note that this summary is not a direct transcription of Agent1’s words but rather an interpretation based solely on their stated rationale.
